rag_rules: Preserve the AADL architecture by inserting AGREE annex blocks only in valid component or implementation scopes
Use implementation scopes for bindings and assignments, and type scopes for assumptions and guarantees
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
generic_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks in the intended target component or implementation scope
Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use valid component or implementation scopes for AGREE annex blocks
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are written inside `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks
Component type annexes are for assumptions, guarantees, constants, and equation declarations
Implementation annexes are for implementation-level bindings such as assignments
anti_patterns: Do not place guarantee statements in implementation scope
Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in a component type annex
Do not use subcomponent ports in component type contracts unless represented through valid variables or outputs
irrelevant_topics: AADL property names as AGREE variables
